scenes.text: In this lecture, we will explore Docker, a platform used to develop, ship, and run applications inside containers. Docker allows developers to package applications with all parts they need, such as libraries and other dependencies, and ship it all out as one package. It provides a standard way to run your code, regardless of where it will be executed. Such setup ensures efficiency, scalability, and consistency across different environments.
scenes.image_prompt: graph LR; A[Developer] --> B[Docker] --> C[Containers] --> D[Applications]; C --> E[Dependencies]; B -- Standardization --> E;
